entries.title: Most people don't know this: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
stories: The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
The opening scene cat was a stray found on the lot. Brando insisted on keeping it. The cat's purring was so loud dialogue had to be re-recorded.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Harry Potter's Real Owls
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.
The DeLorean was chosen because it looked alien-like. The company had gone bankrupt so production bought remaining stock. Only about 9000 were ever made.
An animator admitted to spelling 'SEX' in a dust cloud during the Circle of Life scene. Disney later digitally removed it. The animator said it was a prank.

stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.
An animator admitted to spelling 'SEX' in a dust cloud during the Circle of Life scene. Disney later digitally removed it. The animator said it was a prank.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Jaws' Mechanical Shark
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
stories: The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
James Cameron hired a linguist to create the Na'vi language with over 2000 words and its own grammar. Fans now speak it fluently.
The DeLorean was chosen because it looked alien-like. The company had gone bankrupt so production bought remaining stock. Only about 9000 were ever made.

trivia_titles: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Jurassic Park's T-Rex Vision
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
stories: The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
trivia_titles: Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.
An animator admitted to spelling 'SEX' in a dust cloud during the Circle of Life scene. Disney later digitally removed it. The animator said it was a prank.

trivia_titles: Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: The Dark Knight's Real Hospital
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
stories: The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.
An animator admitted to spelling 'SEX' in a dust cloud during the Circle of Life scene. Disney later digitally removed it. The animator said it was a prank.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Jurassic Park's T-Rex Vision
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The opening scene cat was a stray found on the lot. Brando insisted on keeping it. The cat's purring was so loud dialogue had to be re-recorded.

stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The DeLorean was chosen because it looked alien-like. The company had gone bankrupt so production bought remaining stock. Only about 9000 were ever made.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.
The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
trivia_titles: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Jurassic Park's T-Rex Vision
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Jaws' Mechanical Shark
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
stories: The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
The opening scene cat was a stray found on the lot. Brando insisted on keeping it. The cat's purring was so loud dialogue had to be re-recorded.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.

stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: The Matrix's Bullet Time
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.
The DeLorean was chosen because it looked alien-like. The company had gone bankrupt so production bought remaining stock. Only about 9000 were ever made.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

stories: The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.

The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The opening scene cat was a stray found on the lot. Brando insisted on keeping it. The cat's purring was so loud dialogue had to be re-recorded.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Jurassic Park's T-Rex Vision
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
stories: The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: The Matrix's Bullet Time
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Jurassic Park's T-Rex Vision
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Pulp Fiction's Chronological Order

stories: Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
trivia_titles: Wizard of Oz Used Real Poison
stories: The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.
Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

stories: Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Pulp Fiction's Chronological Order
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived

The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Forrest Gump's History Edits
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Jaws' Mechanical Shark
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: The Matrix's Bullet Time
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.
The Overlook Hotel layout in The Shining is physically impossible. The exterior shows 5 stories but interior corridors don't align. Kubrick did this on purpose to create unease.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

entries.title: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: You won't believe what happened behind the scenes:
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: The Matrix's Bullet Time
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
The opening scene cat was a stray found on the lot. Brando insisted on keeping it. The cat's purring was so loud dialogue had to be re-recorded.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.

trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.
The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.

stories: The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
trivia_titles: Indiana Jones and the Real Gun
stories: In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The exploding hospital scene used real explosives in an abandoned building. The detonation was delayed so Heath Ledger improvised by tapping the detonator repeatedly. Nolan kept it.
James Cameron hired a linguist to create the Na'vi language with over 2000 words and its own grammar. Fans now speak it fluently.

entries.title: This movie fact sounds fake but is true: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: This movie fact sounds fake but is true:
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.

stories: Forrest Gump used CGI to place Tom Hanks into real historical footage. The scene with JFK used groundbreaking face replacement technology that won the film its first Visual Effects Oscar.
Pulp Fiction is told out of order. Vincent Vega dies halfway through the story chronologically. Tarantino wrote it this way so audiences would rewatch it.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.
The owls in Harry Potter were real not CGI. Hedwig was played by multiple male snowy owls. Trainers worked 48-hour shifts to keep them healthy under studio lights.

stories: The Wicked Witch's green makeup contained toxic copper-based paint. Actor Margaret Hamilton suffered second-degree burns when a trapdoor malfunctioned during a fire stunt.

entries.title: Hollywood kept this secret quiet: Titanic's Jack Could Have Lived
entries.hook: Hollywood kept this secret quiet:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: Director James Cameron confirmed Jack could not fit on the door in Titanic. But a 2022 experiment proved both could fit. Cameron later said 'Jack had to die. It's called art not science.'
The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

Frozen's Let It Go Was Almost Cut
stories: The bullet time effect used 120 still cameras in a circle around the actor firing in sequence. The technique was so expensive they only had one chance per shot.
In Raiders of the Lost Ark Indy shoots a swordsman instead of fighting. Harrison Ford was sick with dysentery and suggested 'Just shoot him.' It became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
The song 'Let It Go' was nearly removed from Frozen after test audiences found it 'too Broadway.' It won the Oscar and the film became the highest-grossing animated film ever at release.

entries.title: Most people don't know this: Star Wars Sound Effects
entries.hook: Most people don't know this:
trivia_titles: The Sixth Sense Twist Was Hidden
stories: The lightsaber hum was created from a movie projector combined with TV interference. Blaster sounds came from striking a tight wire. Darth Vader's breathing was a scuba regulator.
The line 'Don't move T-Rex can't see you' is scientifically wrong. Paleontologists confirmed T-Rex had better vision than a hawk. Spielberg kept it because it made the scene more tense.
M Night Shyamalan hid clues throughout The Sixth Sense that Bruce Willis was dead. He never interacts directly with anyone except the boy. Audiences rewatched immediately to spot the clues.

entries.title: This movie secret was hidden for years: Psycho's Toilet Scene Changed Movies
entries.hook: This movie secret was hidden for years:
stories: Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho featured the first toilet flush ever shown on screen. The Hays Code had banned showing toilets but Hitchcock fought for it and broke a 30-year taboo.
The mechanical shark named Bruce kept breaking down. Spielberg had to imply the shark's presence instead of showing it. This 'flaw' is what made the film terrifying.
Mars Inc turned down product placement for E.T. Hershey's agreed to use Reese's Pieces instead. Sales tripled within two weeks making it one of the most successful product placements ever.
